implementedBehavior: runCodexCliExecPlan now defaults to autoProbeModelWithCli=true and requireModelProbe=true whenever the execution plan contains a model and skipExecutionModelProbe is not set.
The default automatic probe runs before the main task spawn and blocks execution if the probe fails.
Callers can still opt out explicitly with skipExecutionModelProbe=true.
Internal probe execution keeps skipExecutionModelProbe=true so the probe does not recurse into itself.

coveredScenarios.name: default_auto_probe
coveredScenarios.result: a modeled execution now performs a strict probe before the main spawn by default

coveredScenarios.name: explicit_skip
coveredScenarios.result: callers can disable the default auto-probe only by setting skipExecutionModelProbe=true

coveredScenarios.name: catalog_gate_coexists
coveredScenarios.result: catalog-based model checks and CLI probe preflight both remain compatible

limitations: The default-execution evidence is covered by injected-spawner tests rather than a new live task execution through a higher-level host.
A later host integration step can still choose whether to surface probe reuse/caching explicitly in UI or startup state.
